accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurately describe the Stafford Act and FTC Act context.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all details trace to the bill text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill prohibits price gouging during declared disasters/emergencies as an unfair/deceptive practice.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly references the Stafford Act and FTC Act provisions the bill invokes.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the material cost-attributable, tariff, seasonal hotel, and rental-repair/contract exceptions.

effective_dates.why: Notes the 30-day, 180-day, and 2-year limitation periods and annual inflation adjustment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the price caps, exceptions, definitions, enforcement, and penalties faithfully.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: FTC, state attorneys general, and the Stafford Act are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The 10%, 50%, 30/180-day, $25,000, triple-damages, and 2-year figures all match the bill.
